metaTitle: Commercial Pest Control NYC | Licensed & Insured
metaDescription: Commercial pest control in NYC. Customized IPM programs for offices, warehouses, and facilities. Licensed. Free inspection. Call now.
h1: Commercial Pest Control in NYC — Custom IPM Programs for Your Business
heroParagraph: Commercial pest control in New York City operates under a different set of requirements than residential service. Commercial properties — office buildings, warehouses, retail locations, schools, healthcare facilities, and multi-unit residential buildings — have specific regulatory compliance requirements, higher stakes for pest sightings, and more complex structural pest pressures than single-family homes. A cockroach in a residential kitchen is a nuisance. A cockroach in a commercial kitchen or medical facility is a health code violation with potential consequences including DOH violations, closure, and reputational damage.
bodyParagraphs: NYC commercial pest management follows an Integrated Pest Management (IPM) protocol mandated for certain facility types including schools and healthcare facilities. IPM emphasizes prevention, monitoring, and minimizing pesticide use — we document every inspection, every pest sighting, every treatment applied, and every recommendation made. For facilities subject to DOH inspection, this documentation demonstrates due diligence and a compliant pest management program. We provide written service reports after every visit, trend analysis by pest type and location, and annual program reviews.

The monthly service model for commercial accounts reflects the reality that commercial pest management is a continuous program, not an event. Large facilities have too many access points, too many staff, too much incoming product, and too much organic activity for a single treatment to provide lasting protection. Monthly inspections catch new activity before it escalates, verify that previous treatments are holding, and allow us to adjust protocols based on seasonal pressure and facility changes.
signs: Pest sightings by staff or customers, particularly in food prep, storage, or customer-facing areas
Evidence of pest activity: droppings, gnaw marks, tracks, nesting material in storage areas
DOH violation for pest evidence or improper pest control documentation
Incoming deliveries of product from warehouse or distribution facilities — common introduction route for rodents, cockroaches, and stored product pests
Facility changes — new construction, new tenants, new product storage — that create new pest harborage or entry opportunities
Current pest vendor not providing written service reports or detailed activity documentation
treatmentApproach: Commercial accounts receive a dedicated service technician, scheduled monthly inspection visits, documented inspection reports with pest activity maps, and written treatment records for regulatory compliance. Service protocol is tailored to the facility type: food service facilities follow specific DOH-compliant protocols for product selection and placement; warehouses focus on rodent exclusion and monitoring stations; healthcare facilities follow strict IPM documentation requirements. We participate in vendor qualification processes and provide all documentation required for DOH, Joint Commission, or other regulatory compliance.

faqs.q: What's included in a monthly commercial pest control contract?
faqs.a: Monthly inspection and treatment visits by a licensed technician, written service reports documenting pest activity and treatments, pheromone monitoring traps and rodent bait stations where applicable, priority response for emergency pest activity between scheduled visits, and quarterly trend reports. Contract terms are customized to facility type.

faqs.q: Do you provide DOH-compliant pest control for NYC restaurants?
faqs.a: Yes. We're familiar with NYC DOH inspection criteria for pest evidence. Our commercial restaurant program includes documentation practices designed to demonstrate compliance — treatment records, product labels, applicator license information — all available for DOH review on request.

faqs.q: Can you take over from our current pest control company?
faqs.a: Yes. We conduct an initial walkthrough inspection to assess current pest pressure, identify any active issues the previous vendor missed, and establish baseline monitoring before beginning monthly service. Transition is seamless — no service gap.

faqs.q: How much does commercial pest control cost in NYC?
faqs.a: Commercial service starts at $175/month for small offices or retail locations and scales to $1,500+/month for large facilities with complex pest pressures. Pricing is based on facility size, pest pressure level, service frequency, and documentation requirements. We provide a written proposal after the initial assessment.

faqs.q: How do you handle pest control in facilities that operate 24/7?
faqs.a: We regularly service facilities with continuous operations — distribution centers, hospitals, data centers, and 24-hour food service operations. Scheduling is coordinated with your facility manager to identify the lowest-impact windows (typically overnight or shift changes) and our technicians work quickly and without disrupting operations. We carry all required insurance and can provide certificates of insurance for vendor compliance requirements.

faqs.q: What documentation do you provide for commercial clients?
faqs.a: We provide detailed service reports after every visit, including areas treated, products applied (with SDS sheets available), and any pest activity observed. For clients in regulated industries, we can provide documentation formatted for DOH inspections, third-party audits, or internal compliance records. All services are logged in your account portal, giving you a full audit trail on demand.
